judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 fully satisfies all four expectations, including explicitly endorsing same-commit doc updates going forward and recommending dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the code; Report 2 matches on (a) and (b) but explicitly rejects bundling the doc fix into the refund PR (missing the "same commit/PR" endorsement) and merely corrects the webhook count to 5 rather than recommending removal of the hardcoded number.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py:1-17`... The code does not follow that pattern everywhere today. I found two undocumented violations... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14`... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9`... `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6-9` is the one legitimate, documented exception

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... Two places call `chargekit` directly instead of going through `gateway.py`: `backend/app/api/invoices.py:4`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2`... `billing/errors.py` is the one documented exception (imports only `chargekit.errors`, not the client)

report_1.evidence: `InvoiceOut` in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has six fields — the doc's five plus `external_ref` — which was added in commit `c23da88`

report_2.evidence: The actual `InvoiceOut` dataclass (`models/invoice.py:16-22`) and its `from_model` constructor (`:24-28`) include a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is populated by the charge route (`invoices.py:17`...) and backed by a real migrated column (`migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`)

report_1.evidence: Whichever refund implementation lands, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` should be updated in the same commit that adds the refund call and any webhook handling for it (see #4) — not deferred.

report_2.evidence: the doc should be corrected now, before refund support is designed on top of it — not bundled into the refund PR as an incidental fix.

report_1.evidence: drop the count and describe the property instead (e.g. "the webhook handler in `app/api/webhooks.py` is the source of truth for which event types are handled — check there rather than trusting a count here"), since as soon as refund support adds any new event type... a hardcoded number will drift again immediately.

report_2.evidence: The doc's count and enumeration are both wrong — it should read "5 webhook event types" and list `payment_pending` alongside the other four.
